headline: Gianni Infantino, the controversial emperor of football business
description: The head of world football will kick off the 2026 World Cup alongside Donald Trump on June 11, launching a tournament of unprecedented scale. More countries, more matches, more revenue: The event will reflect the strategy he has pursued since taking over at the helm of FIFA 10 years ago, to grow the organization's business to enrich national federations and, above all, to strengthen his own grip on power.

headline: Killing of 11-year-old Lyhanna: 'People criticize the justice system for being slow, but the real issue is the resources it is given to move quickly'
The allegations against French singer Patrick Bruel, charged with rape, sexual assault
'Speaking with Putin? For Europe, there may be a window of opportunity. But how and for what purpose?'
description: On the eve of three major summits – the G7 in Evian, the European Council in Brussels, and the NATO summit in Ankara – Europe is positioning itself to take the lead in potential talks on Ukraine's future as Donald Trump remains mired in the Middle East, explains columnist Sylvie Kauffmann.

headline: Iran calls ceasefire 'practically meaningless' after renewed US strikes
description: Iran's new body overseeing the Strait of Hormuz also confirmed on Thursday a complete closure order for the strategic waterway until further notice.

headline: Charlie Dalin, winner of the 2025 Vendée Globe sailing race, dies at 42
description: The French sailor won the Vendée Globe, a solo, non-stop, unassisted round-the-world race, finishing over nine days and eight hours ahead of the previous record, all while battling cancer for two years. His family announced his death on June 11.

headline: How a brilliant French engineer joined IS with her children
description: Camille F. said her radicalization grew out of the wounds of her privileged upbringing and her intense love for her husband. Still trapped in denial, she struggled to explain to the court how she ended up taking her children to what she described as a near 'certain' death in Syria. She was sentenced on Wednesday to 12 years in prison.

'While the G7 is indisputably obsolescent, nothing seems capable of replacing it'
description: Although rooted in a bygone era and battered by Donald Trump, the Group of Seven continues to meet purely out of habit, writes Le Monde columnist Gilles Paris, one week before the summit chaired by French President Emmanuel Macron in Evian.

headline: 'Disclosure Day': Steven Spielberg goes back to the future
description: After two more personal, retrospective feature films, the American filmmaker revisits his passion for extraterrestrials.

headline: 'Operation Pushkin': Rare books, Georgian con artists and the shadow of the Kremlin
description: For two years, Georgian criminals orchestrated the theft of rare editions of works by Alexander Pushkin from major European libraries. The trial of five of them begins in Paris on June 9. It could reveal whether Russia was involved.
